---
title: "Simple Data Visualization Example"
author: "Allissa Dillman PhD, Lisa Karstens PhD, Juliette Kudrick"
date: "2026-07-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Purpose

This script will read the contents of `pheno_select.csv` into RStudio and produce a simple data visualization looking at VO2 max data from the MoTrPAC dataset.

## Load Libraries

First, we load all of the libraries necessary for this analysis.

**Note:** If you do not already have `ggplot2` or `here` installed on your machine, you will need to run `install.packages("ggplot2")` and `install.packages("here")` before running the `library` calls.

```{r load_lib}

library(ggplot2)
library(here)

```

## Read Data

Next, we read in `pheno_select.csv`, a csv file containing MoTrPAC data.

```{r read_data}

pheno_select <- read.csv(file = here("06_intro_to_R", "pheno_select.csv"))

```

## Examine Data

Below we run a couple simple summary functions to make sure that we have the correct data.

```{r eda}

## dimensions of the object
nrow(pheno_select)
ncol(pheno_select)

## number of unique participants
length(unique(pheno_select$pid))

## what variables do we have?
colnames(pheno_select)
```

## Example Visualization

Below, we create a simple histogram using `ggplot`.

```{r example_viz}

ggplot(pheno_select, aes(x = vo_max1)) + geom_histogram()

```

## Your Turn!

Building off of the plot above, complete the following:

-   Facet by sex of the mouse (call `facet_wrap()`
-   Fill in bars based on sex (`fill = sex`)

```{r student_viz}

ggplot(pheno_select, aes(x = vo_max1, fill = sex)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  facet_wrap(~sex)

```

Now that you have completed this data visualization task, save the rmd as 
<yourname>_viz.Rmd. This file can by pushed to your repository.
